Use the `ListItemStyle` object to define text formatting for a bulleted (unordered) or numbered (ordered) list. # ListItemStyle

**Object**

The object for defining the style for bulleted or numbered lists in an article.

## Declaration

```swift
object ListItemStyle
```

## Discussion

Use the `ListItemStyle` object to define text formatting for a bulleted (unordered) or numbered (ordered) list. Put `listItemStyle` inside a [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TextStyle] object or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentTextStyle] object and set the text component’s `format` property to `html`. See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format].

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TextStyle] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentTextStyle].

### Example

```json
{
  "componentTextStyles": {
    "exampleStyle": {
      …
      "unorderedListItems": {
        "type": "character",
        "character": "✓"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Text Styles

- **Defining and Applying Text Styles**: Define and apply custom, default, and inline text styles, or use HTML tags or Markdown syntax to style your text.
- **Applying Apple News Format Fonts**: Choose a font family for Apple News Format that’s supported in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS.
- **TextStyle**: The object for defining the text style, such as font family, size, and color, that you can apply to ranges of text.
- **ComponentTextStyle**: The object for defining the style for a text component, including spacing, alignment, and drop caps.
- **DropCapStyle**: The object for defining the drop cap text style to use in the first paragraph in a text component.
- **InlineTextStyle**: The object for applying text styling when not using HTML or Markdown formatting.
